figures.caption: Table 7. CoC flip as an alarm signal across all eight conditions (alarm = CoC changed; unsafe event = L2 deviation >5m, n= 1,996 per attack). Precision remains ≥0.79 throughout; recall and AUROC degrade under severe noise as language and trajectory branches decouple.

Table 8. Enabling CoC generation is associated with reduced ADE by 11.8% on average, consistent across every condition including all eight attacks (mean Cohen’s dz=0.14; all p < 0.0001, paired t-test with Wilcoxon confirmation).

Figure 1. ADE grows linearly with noise intensity (R2 = 0.957, slope = 0.0048m/σ; four noise levels), enabling a direct riskassessment formula: each 10-unit σ increment costs ≈ 0.05m ADE. Error bars: 95% CI.
